qualifying_org_definition: Any community-based organization where the individual performs self-initiated community service, explicitly including churches, food banks, immigrant support centers, youth programs, and other nonprofits — no pre-approved list, MOU, or provider registration required for the self-initiated community service lane (provider involvement is required only for the distinct Community Workfare lane).
verification_instrument: Form IL444-2610 (SNAP Activity Report) — given to an organization representative to verify hours, sign, and date; alternatively, a signed letter from the organization confirming volunteer hours is accepted. Recipient uploads completed documentation via Manage My Case (MMC) portal or submits to local Family Community Resource Center. The Serve Illinois Galaxy Digital 'Benefit Activity Report' is also accepted as an optional alternative channel, but is not required.
hours_formula_detail: Form IL444-2610 states explicitly: '20 hours per week (80 hours monthly)' — Illinois follows the federal 7 CFR 273.24 baseline with no workfare-formula deviation.
accepts_outofstate_detail: Illinois policy requires 'any community-based organization' to sign Form IL444-2610 — no geographic restriction on where the organization is incorporated or headquartered. IDHS policy and the IL444-2610 instructions contain no in-state registration or Illinois nexus requirement. Under federal default (7 CFR 273.24), any qualifying nonprofit is acceptable absent a state restriction; Illinois has not imposed one.
geographic_waivers: none — fully enforced statewide. Illinois ended its prior statewide ABAWD waiver effective February 2026 (MR#26.03). The OBBBA (signed July 4, 2025) terminated all remaining FNA-approved ABAWD time-limit waivers no later than November 2, 2026; as of June 2026 the FNA lists no active ABAWD waivers for Illinois. Cook County and Chicago area historically had waivers, but those are no longer operative.
submission_deadline: Form must be returned 'in a timely manner' per IL444-2610 instructions — no specific calendar deadline stated. Hours are verified on a monthly basis per the agency certification language. Recipient is responsible for returning the completed form to the FCRC each month.
notes:
Illinois is Tier 1 clean: IDHS policy explicitly allows self-initiated community service at 'any community-based organization' (including nonprofits, churches, food banks) with no mandatory EDP, county assignment, or provider registration as a prestep. Community Workfare (county-placed, requires an E&T provider) is a separate, distinct lane. Verification uses Form IL444-2610 (SNAP Activity Report) signed by the org representative, or a signed letter from the org — the recipient then self-reports hours by uploading the form. Illinois enforced ABAWD time limits beginning February 2026 after ending its statewide waiver (MR#26.03). IDHS administers SNAP statewide through Family Community Resource Centers, so county_variation_flag is false.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Conducted adversarial multi-source search to attempt to refute the Tier 1 classification. Four threat vectors were investigated: (1) 'Work provisions' registration — Illinois SNAP has a general work provisions requirement (ages 15-59, formerly called 'work registration') where recipients must report employment status at application/redetermination. Confirmed via IDHS item=175082 that this is separate from and does not gate the ABAWD volunteer lane; SNAP E&T participation is explicitly voluntary and self-initiated community service requires no case manager assignment or EDP. (2) Serve Illinois Galaxy Digital — IDHS promotes the Serve Illinois platform as a way to find volunteer opportunities; confirmed via the Organization Reference Guide that Galaxy Digital's Benefit Activity Report is one of two accepted reporting options, the other being the IL444-2610. Galaxy Digital is NOT a required channel and organizations do not need to register there for hours to count. (3) Pre-approved site list / MOU requirement — No evidence found of any approved organization list or MOU requirement for the self-initiated community service lane. The only requirement is that an organizational representative signs the IL444-2610 or provides a confirming letter. (4) E&T State Plan gating — FNS page timed out; the SNAP E&T manual PDF (illinoisworknet.com) did not disclose additional gating for the self-initiated lane. MR#26.03 confirms 'self-initiated Community Service counts as unpaid work' with no described prestep. All four adversarial angles failed to find blocking steps. Tier 1 classification confirmed. Remote stance remains 'unknown' — policy language references community-based organizations but does not explicitly address remote/virtual volunteering. Confidence held at 'high' based on direct IDHS policy manual language (item=175082, MR#26.03), Illinois Legal Aid explainer, and We Got You Illinois, all consistently showing no mandatory prestep for the self-initiated community service lane.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): (1) Hours formula — Form IL444-2610 text explicitly states '20 hours per week (80 hours monthly)'; Illinois follows the federal_80 baseline with no workfare-formula deviation. (2) Out-of-state nonprofit — No in-state registration or Illinois nexus requirement found anywhere in IDHS policy, MR#26.03, the IL444-2610 form, or the Serve Illinois guidance. Policy refers to 'any community-based organization.' Classified as 'silent' — federal default applies, so out-of-state 501(c)(3) should be operationally accepted. (3) Geographic waivers — Illinois statewide waiver ended February 2026 per MR#26.03; OBBBA terminated all remaining FNA ABAWD waivers; FNA lists no active IL waivers as of June 2026. Fully enforced statewide. (4) Submission UX — IL444-2610 instructions allow MMC online portal upload, mail to FCRC, in-person at FCRC, or mail to Central Scanning Unit. Reporting is monthly per the agency certification language ('verify on a monthly basis'). No specific calendar deadline cited; form must be returned 'in a timely manner.' (5) Form fields — Extracted from IL444-2610 PDF text: requires org_name, org_address (city/state/zip), org_phone (and/or email), org_signature, supervisor_name (Authorized Contact Name), recipient_name, recipient_case_number, hours_total (80 hrs/month), hours_by_day_or_week (20 hrs/week), period_start_date (beginning date). No EIN or notarization required.
